phase_1.audio: Acoustic analysis reveals baseline of 69.4 dBA with periodic spikes up to 104.4 dBA throughout the 418s recording. Very loud environment detected; concentration significantly compromised.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 66 bpm throughout session (baseline typical for calm, focused state). Data indicates user was in optimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS positioning shows initial variance of 4.0m, refining to 3.0m accuracy over session duration. Accelerometer readings show user quickly becoming immobile and remaining seated throughout.

phase_2.correlations: The shift toward sustained stillness aligns with GPS signal stabilization. Audio channel demonstrates notable transitions.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session shows moderate concentration quality with mixed environmental factors. Acoustic baseline of 81.4 dB provides challenging conditions. User maintained consistent positioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Invest in noise-canceling technology to mitigate high ambient levels.

reason: Score weighted toward audio environment (81.4dB—loud), stable positioning, and session duration (418s). Overall favorable conditions.
